seo.title: Arizona Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Arizona — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license arizona, dental assistant certification arizona, RDA arizona, DANB CDA arizona, dental assistant radiography arizona

board.name: Arizona State Board of Dental Examiners

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Arizona.

steps.title: Complete Education/Training
steps.description: Complete a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or gain on-the-job training under a licensed dentist.

steps.title: Obtain Required Certifications
steps.description: Obtain DANB CDA certification or pass state-specific exams as required.

steps.title: Apply for State Credential
steps.description: No state application required for general dental assistants. Check expanded function requirements.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Arizona does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Arizona require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Arizona does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Arizona?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Arizona.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Arizona?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Arizona?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Arizona. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Arizona does not license general dental assistants. Certification required for expanded functions like radiography and coronal polishing.
